meaning: adj.死气沉沉的,没生命的,单调的
examples: A rock is an inanimate object.
Many children become attached to some inanimate object, such as a blanket or soft toy.
Natural philosophy involved the study of all aspects of the material world, animate and inanimate.
Participants either saw live footage of themselves, a mannequin or an inanimate black block.
Rather, it asserted that cellphones are analogous to other mundane inanimate objects, like cigarette boxes and clothes.
